events.title: Caribbean Petroleum refinery fuel-oil leak reaches Las Lajas Creek and nearby marshland (Cataño, Puerto Rico)
events.description: An EPA report says a storage tank leak at the Caribbean Petroleum refinery released more than 16,000 gallons of fuel oil, with more than 800 gallons reaching Las Lajas Creek. The creek is described as a tributary to the Ciénaga Las Cucharillas marshland, and the spill was discovered on September 22, 1999 after occurring on September 21.

sources.headline: Caribbean Petroleum spill reaches Las Lajas Creek

key_quotes.quote: released more than 16,000 gallons of fuel oil; more than 800 gallons ... reached Las Lajas Creek

events.title: Forklift drop spills sodium hypochlorite (bleach solution) at a plant; NRC notified (Mississippi)
events.description: An NRC event report states a 400-gallon container of 12% sodium hypochlorite was dropped from a forklift and about 325 gallons spilled in a parking lot. The report indicates the spill was contained and the National Response Center was notified.

key_quotes.quote: 400-gallon container of 12% sodium hypochlorite ... dropped off a forklift and ~325 gallons spilled

events.title: Raw sewage release reported to Hudson River (New York)
events.description: An NRC event report describes an offsite notification for a release of less than 10 gallons of sewage that entered a storm drain discharging to the Hudson River. The report indicates the source was secured and the drain was diked and cleaned.

key_quotes.quote: release of less than ten gallons of sewage ... to a storm drain discharging to the Hudson River

events.title: Hurricane Floyd causes catastrophic rainfall and inland flooding in North Carolina and the U.S. Mid-Atlantic
events.description: Hurricane Floyd produced torrential rainfall that led to widespread, catastrophic inland flooding, notably in eastern North Carolina, after moving from the Bahamas to U.S. landfall near Cape Fear on September 16, 1999. Flood impacts continued as the system progressed northward along the U.S. East Coast.

sources.headline: USGS fact sheet (FS 073-01) on Hurricane Floyd flooding (September–October 1999)
Effects of Hurricane Floyd inland flooding (September–October 1999)

key_quotes.quote: Hurricane Floyd ... produced torrential rainfall leading to widespread flooding in eastern North Carolina.

dedupe.cluster_hint: Hurricane Floyd inland flooding North Carolina Sep 1999

events.title: Typhoon York impacts Hong Kong and makes landfall near Zhuhai, bringing destructive winds and flooding
events.description: Typhoon York affected Hong Kong in September 1999, prompting the hoisting of the No. 10 signal, and later made landfall near Zhuhai in Guangdong. The storm brought strong winds and heavy rain associated with flooding impacts in the region.

key_quotes.quote: No. 10 Hurricane Signal was hoisted during the passage of Typhoon York.

dedupe.cluster_hint: Typhoon York Hong Kong Zhuhai Sep 1999

events.title: Hurricane Greg brings heavy rain to Baja California Sur (draft source too generic)
events.description: The draft states that Hurricane Greg made landfall in Baja California Sur in early September 1999 and produced heavy rain and flooding impacts. The only provided source is a general NOAA NHC “past storms” index page, which does not by itself substantiate the specific impacts described.

dedupe.cluster_hint: Hurricane Greg Baja California Sur Sep 1999

events.title: Typhoon Bart brings heavy rainfall and related flooding/landslide impacts in Japan
events.description: Typhoon Bart (1999) affected Japan in late September, with very heavy rainfall totals reported in parts of the country. The draft describes flooding and landslide impacts associated with the storm as it moved across Japan after landfall in Kyushu.

events.title: Tropical Storm Zia affects Japan with heavy rain (draft source too generic)
events.description: The draft states that Tropical Storm Zia (9916) made landfall in Japan on September 14, 1999 and brought heavy rain contributing to flooding and landslide-related disruptions. The only provided source is a general NOAA NHC “past storms” index page, which does not substantiate these specific details on its own.

events.title: Chi-Chi (Jiji) earthquake in central Taiwan (1999)
events.description: A major earthquake struck central Taiwan near Jiji/Chi-Chi in Nantou County on 1999-09-20. Sources describe rupture of the Chelungpu fault system and severe impacts with significant aftershock activity.

location.name: Central Taiwan (near Jiji/Chi-Chi, Nantou County), Taiwan

events.title: Oaxaca earthquake in southern Mexico (1999)
events.description: A major earthquake struck Oaxaca state in southern Mexico on 1999-09-30, affecting the San Baltazar Loxicha / Puerto Escondido region. The referenced source describes a strong damaging event in the area.

location.name: Oaxaca state, Mexico (near San Baltazar Loxicha / Puerto Escondido region)

events.title: Willow Fire near Lake Arrowhead reported contained on Sep 5, 1999
events.description: NASA imagery documented the Willow Fire burning in Southern California on September 1, 1999. Reporting indicated the fire near the Lake Arrowhead area was declared fully contained on September 5, 1999.

location.name: Lake Arrowhead area, San Bernardino County, California, USA

sources.headline: Willow Fire in Southern California's San Bernardino County

key_quotes.quote: Willow Fire in Southern California's San Bernardino County

events.title: Bridge Fire reported burning above Glendora on Sep 2, 1999
events.description: A wildfire referred to as the Bridge Fire was reported burning in San Gabriel Canyon above Glendora in the Angeles National Forest. Coverage described limited containment as of September 2, 1999 amid broader regional fire activity.

location.name: San Gabriel Canyon above Glendora (Angeles National Forest), Los Angeles County, California, USA

dedupe.cluster_hint: bridge fire glendora angeles national forest sep 1999

events.title: Northern California fires and smoke documented by NASA on Sep 2, 1999
events.description: NASA Earth Observatory reported that multiple fire groups in Northern California were producing widespread smoke. The summary noted nearly 45,000 acres burning in areas associated with the Plumas and Shasta-Trinity National Forest regions on September 2, 1999.

location.name: Plumas and Shasta-Trinity National Forest regions, Northern California, USA

dedupe.cluster_hint: northern california fires plumas shasta trinity sep 2 1999

events.title: NASA map summarized global large-fire detections for September 1999 (TRMM VIRS)
events.description: NASA Earth Observatory published a satellite-derived map showing the distribution of large fires for September 1999 using TRMM VIRS data. The product summarized fire detections across tropical and subtropical regions during the month.

dedupe.cluster_hint: TRMM VIRS global fires September 1999 map

events.title: West Nile virus identified in birds amid increased bird deaths in New York City area
events.description: During investigation of the 1999 encephalitis outbreak in New York City, West Nile virus was identified in avian specimens (including crows) after reports of increased bird deaths. The finding prompted expanded wildlife and mosquito surveillance as part of the public health response.

location.name: New York City, New York, USA

sources.headline: Update: West Nile-Like Viral Encephalitis -- New York, 1999
West Nile fever in the United States of America
Update: West Nile Virus Encephalitis -- New York, 1999

key_quotes.quote: West Nile virus was identified from tissue samples from birds.
Reports of increased bird deaths, particularly among crows, were received.

dedupe.cluster_hint: West Nile virus bird deaths New York City 1999

events.title: Canada grants Royal Assent to Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999)
events.description: Canada’s Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999) received Royal Assent on September 14, 1999. The act updated the federal framework for pollution prevention and protection of the environment and human health.

sources.headline: Canadian Environmental Protection Act passed Parliament (September 1999)

key_quotes.quote: An Act respecting pollution prevention and the protection of the environment and human health

events.title: US EPA directly approves Maryland Clean Air Act 111(d) plan for existing MSW landfills
events.description: The US Environmental Protection Agency issued a direct final rule approving Maryland’s Clean Air Act section 111(d) plan to control emissions from existing municipal solid waste landfills. The approval established enforceable requirements for landfill-related air pollution controls under the state plan.

sources.headline: Approval and Promulgation of Air Quality Implementation Plans; Maryland; Control of Existing Municipal Solid Waste Landfills

key_quotes.quote: direct final rule approving the State plan submitted by Maryland under section 111(d)

events.title: US EPA issues Project XL final rule for hazardous waste management in participating university labs (MA/VT)
events.description: The US Environmental Protection Agency issued a final rule under RCRA providing site-specific regulatory flexibility for hazardous waste management in participating university laboratories. The rule applied to participating institutions including UMass Boston, Boston College, and the University of Vermont as described in the Federal Register notice.

sources.headline: Hazardous Waste Management System; Modification of the Hazardous Waste Program; Project XL Site-Specific Rulemaking for University Laboratories

key_quotes.quote: Project XL Site-Specific Rulemaking for University Laboratories

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA Project XL university laboratories MA VT

events.title: US EPA finalizes hazardous waste combustor MACT/NESHAP standards
events.description: The US Environmental Protection Agency promulgated final standards limiting hazardous air pollutants from hazardous waste combustors, including incinerators, cement kilns, and lightweight aggregate kilns. The standards set limits for pollutants such as dioxins/furans, metals, acid gases, organics, and particulate matter under Clean Air Act and RCRA authorities.

sources.headline: National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants From Hazardous Waste Combustors
Hazardous Waste Combustors: National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants

key_quotes.quote: National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants From Hazardous Waste Combustors
